parser.add_argument("--head-dim", type=int, default=128, help="target head dimension for attention")
 parser.add_argument("--max-seq-len", type=int, default=2048, help="max context length")
 parser.add_argument("--window-pattern", type=str, default="SSSL", help="sliding window pattern tiled across layers: L=full, S=half context (e.g. 'SSL')")
+parser.add_argument("--sparse-funnel", action="store_true", help="use sparse funnel architecture instead of window pattern")
+parser.add_argument("--n-global", type=int, default=-1, help="number of global (full-context) layers (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--chirp-gamma", type=float, default=-1.0, help="chirp exponent for global layer placement (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--local-window", type=int, default=-1, help="local attention window size (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--global-layers", type=str, default="", help="explicit global layer indices, comma-separated")
+parser.add_argument("--local-mlp-ratio", type=int, default=-1, help="MLP ratio for local layers when sparse funnel is enabled (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--rope-base", type=int, default=-1, help="RoPE base frequency (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--bigram-vocab-size", type=int, default=-1, help="hashed bigram embedding buckets (-1 = baked-in sparse default, 0 disables)")
+parser.add_argument("--bigram-dim", type=int, default=-1, help="bigram embedding dim before projection (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--gated-attn", dest="gated_attn", action="store_true", help="enable lightweight per-head attention output gates")
+parser.add_argument("--no-gated-attn", dest="gated_attn", action="store_false", help="disable lightweight per-head attention output gates")
+parser.set_defaults(gated_attn=None)
+parser.add_argument("--attn-gate-channels", type=int, default=-1, help="embedding channels used to predict attention gates (-1 = baked-in sparse default)")
+parser.add_argument("--attn-gate-layers", type=str, default="", help="explicit attention-gate layer indices, comma-separated")
+parser.add_argument("--ve-layers", type=str, default="", help="explicit value-residual layer indices, comma-separated")

discussion_comments.comment_body: they are set now at the defaults, can remove if not needed and bake directly into the config

As stated in the readme, nanochat shouldn't become a configuration monster, so we probably don't want to merge a whole new Python module of 31 lines just to set 3 variables 🙃 

I think we can probably do without `ENABLE_PERIODIC_CLEANUP` and set `ENABLE_FINAL_CLEANUP` always to `True`?

`CACHE_CLEANUP_INTERVAL` can just be defined in `core_eval.py`.

commented_code:
BASE_URL = "https://huggingface.co/datasets/karpathy/fineweb-edu-100b-shuffle/resolve/main"
 MAX_SHARD = 1822 # the last datashard is shard_01822.parquet
 index_to_filename = lambda index: f"shard_{index:05d}.parquet" # format of the filenames
+
+# Always use a fixed shard for val so that the metrics don't depend
+# on how many shards are downloaded
+VAL_SHARD_INDEX = MAX_SHARD

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -23,6 +23,12 @@
 BASE_URL = "https://huggingface.co/datasets/karpathy/fineweb-edu-100b-shuffle/resolve/main"
 MAX_SHARD = 1822 # the last datashard is shard_01822.parquet
 index_to_filename = lambda index: f"shard_{index:05d}.parquet" # format of the filenames
+
+# Always use a fixed shard for val so that the metrics don't depend
+# on how many shards are downloaded
+VAL_SHARD_INDEX = MAX_SHARD
discussion_comments.comment_body:
I personally wouldn't link these two variables to eachother, because the risk would be that in the future for some reason `MAX_SHARD` is changed into a different value without realising the consequences for the validation step. You may as well could do `VAL_SHARD_INDEX=1822`.

I think I'd personally just pick shard 0, which will always be downloaded anyway. This might reduce some if-else logic. The downside being that we won't be able to compare future evaluations with previous ones as soon as we'd merge this.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -23,6 +23,12 @@
 BASE_URL = "https://huggingface.co/datasets/karpathy/fineweb-edu-100b-shuffle/resolve/main"
 MAX_SHARD = 1822 # the last datashard is shard_01822.parquet
 index_to_filename = lambda index: f"shard_{index:05d}.parquet" # format of the filenames
+
+# Always use a fixed shard for val so that the metrics don't depend
+# on how many shards are downloaded
+VAL_SHARD_INDEX = MAX_SHARD
discussion_comments.comment_body: good point - i've decoupled these. `VAL_SHARD_INDEX` is now pinned to 1822. I’m keeping it at shard_01822 specifically for backwards comparability.

you can allow only "mps" or "cuda" by setting `choices=['mps', 'cuda']`
(see: https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#choices)
